test.command: bun test --max-concurrency=6 apps/dashboard/src apps/api/src packages/api/src packages/auth/src packages/db/src packages/env/src

typecheck.command: bun run check-types && bun x tsc -p apps/dashboard/tsconfig.json --noEmit && bun x tsc -p packages/api/tsconfig.json --noEmit && bun x tsc -p packages/auth/tsconfig.json --noEmit && bun x tsc -p packages/db/tsconfig.json --noEmit && bun x tsc -p packages/env/tsconfig.json --noEmit

lint.command: bun x ultracite check apps/dashboard/src apps/api/src packages/api/src packages/auth/src packages/db/src packages/env/src

blockingIssues.description: The fix blocks ZZZ/ZZ but still accepts other non-canonical values because packages/api/src/canonical-codes.ts includes entries such as countryCode="EU" and baseCurrency="AQD". onboardingInputSchema therefore still accepts invalid-but-shaped onboarding codes, so the original onboarding validation gap remains unresolved.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Make `midday-reference` conditionally required for narrow shared-schema/test regressions, or split those fixes from browser/Midday-architecture tasks so workers are not forced through unrelated Midday-reference steps.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The re-review for onboarding-canonical-code-validation found a schema-and-tests-only fix session with no routing/UI changes, but the current dashboard-parity-worker procedure still hard-requires `midday-reference`; the transcript skeleton for worker session 965e8ed3-b627-4dc2-bd83-fbf0e78c4d6f showed no `midday-reference` invocation.

rejectedObservations.observation: The dashboard-parity-worker/skill-compliance observation about `followedProcedure: true` despite missing `ultracite` skill invocation reappeared in the return_to fix review.
rejectedObservations.reason: already-documented in round 1 synthesis under skill compliance reporting; this round did not add a distinct new remediation beyond confirming the same issue persists
